workLog.detail: Triaged open priority issues via gh api (skill-first guard blocks gh issue list; mapping is broken, filed #2110). Selected #1881 as PR 1: fully specified repro, pure regex fix, near-zero blast radius, highest ROI tracer-bullet.

scripts/validation/pr_description.py: widened _EXT_BOUNDARY from (?![A-Za-z0-9_/\\]) to (?![A-Za-z0-9_/\\]|\.[A-Za-z0-9]). Rejects a following dotted alphanumeric segment (double extension) while preserving the sentence-ending-period carve-out from #1874 and full capture of genuine compound names (tsconfig.spec.json).

tests/test_validation_pr_description.py TestExtensionBoundary: added double-extension rejection (bak/orig/example), .tar.gz family, compound-with-earlier-recognized-segment, multiline coexistence, sentence-period and EOF carve-out regression guards. 170 passed (was 165). Sibling files test_pr_description.py + test_pr_description_guard.py: 36 passed.

critic / qa / security agents run in parallel. All APPROVE. Non-blocking findings (strengthen negative assertions to == [], add compound + multiline + tar.gz coverage) folded into the same commit. QA surfaced a pre-existing markdown-link-target extraction gap, filed as follow-up #2113.

Per user standing order, filed issues for every broken guard worked around: #2110 (issue-list mapping points at single-issue script), #2111 (guard regex scans quoted argument text, false-positive blocks non-gh commands).

WENT WELL: tracer-bullet selection proved the gated pipeline end-to-end; parallel three-agent review converged fast on the same cheap hardening; empirical regex tracing before edit caught the sentence-period edge. IMPROVE: first sentence-period test used bare-prose (no FILE_MENTION_PATTERN matches it) and failed; should have checked the four patterns before asserting. LESSONS: bare-prose file mentions are never extracted; only backtick/bold/list/link forms are. Skill-first guard has two defects that cost setup time; both filed. TIME: discovery ~10m, impl ~5m, tests ~10m, review ~6m (parallel), retro/log ~5m.

nextSteps: Commit (atomic, <=5 files), push, open PR referencing Fixes #1881
Run /pr-review on the new PR; resolve feedback
